LinkNest

₹14,999 ₹18,999

Submit Your Affiliate Product

Affiliate disclosure: Links may earn commission • LinkNest 2026

Great — I can build a Flipkart‑like e‑commerce website. To make sure I deliver what you want, a few quick questions first: 1. Must‑have features for your MVP (pick/check): user auth, product catalog, search, filtering, product pages, cart, checkout (payments), orders, seller/admin panel, ratings/reviews, recommendations? 2. Preferred tech stack or constraints (React/Next.js, Vue, plain HTML, backend language: Node/Express, Django, Rails, DB: PostgreSQL/MySQL/Firebase)? 3. Do you need real payment integration (Stripe/PayPal/Paytm) now or just a mock checkout? 4. Target launch environment (Vercel + Railway, DigitalOcean, AWS)? 5. Do you want me to open a PR in your repo lavk7251-hue/LinkNest- and push starter code there? Below I propose an MVP plan, architecture, timeline, and a small starter scaffold (full‑stack) you can run locally. MVP (minimum viable product) — core features - Public product catalog with categories, images, prices, discounts - Product detail page with description, images, reviews - Search + basic filtering (category, price) - User accounts (signup/login) - Cart and checkout flow (mock payment or real gateway) - Order history for users - Admin dashboard to add/approve products and view orders Recommended tech stack (scalable and common) - Frontend: React (Vite) or Next.js (if you want SSR/SEO). I’ll use React + Vite for the starter. - Backend: Node.js + Express (REST API) - Database: PostgreSQL (relational for orders/products). Use Sequelize/Prisma for ORM. - Cache/search: Redis for sessions & cart, ElasticSearch or PostgreSQL full‑text for search (start with Postgres text search). - Storage: S3 (or S3‑compatible) for product images - Payments: Stripe (international) or Paytm/Razorpay (India) - Hosting: Vercel for frontend, Railway/Heroku/AWS for backend, PlanetScale/Render etc. for DB High‑level architecture - Client (React) <-> REST API (Express) <-> PostgreSQL + Redis - Images uploaded to S3, publicly served via CDN - Optional: worker queue (Bull) for background tasks (emails, thumbnail generation) - Admin panel (same frontend, protected routes) Estimated timeline (MVP) - 1–2 days: Project scaffold + basic product catalog + static pages - 3–7 days: Auth, cart, checkout (mock), product CRUD, admin UI - 1–2 days: Payment integration, email notifications - Additional: search, recommendations, analytics, hardening Starter scaffold Below is a minimal full‑stack starter you can clone/run. It gives a product listing, product detail, cart (client state), and a simple Express API that serves product data. README (how to run) ````markdown name=README.md # LinkNest Starter (Flipkart-like MVP) This is a minimal starter scaffold (React frontend + Express backend). Prereqs - Node.js 18+ - npm or pnpm Run locally 1. Start backend cd server npm install npm run dev (server runs at http://localhost:4000) 2. Start frontend cd client npm install npm run dev (client runs at http://localhost:5173) What's included - /server: Express API with in-memory product list (replace with Postgres) - /client: React + Vite app showing product listing, product detail, simple cart Next steps - Replace in-memory store with PostgreSQL + ORM - Add user auth (JWT/session) - Add payments (Stripe) - Add admin UI and image uploads ```` Server: minimal Express API ```javascript name=server/index.js const express = require('express') const cors = require('cors') const app = express() app.use(cors()) app.use(express.json()) // In-memory products (replace with DB) let products = [ { id: 1, name: '5G Smartphone', category: 'mobile', price: 14999, oldPrice: 18999, badge: '20% OFF', image: 'https://via.placeholder.com/600x400?text=Phone', rating: 4 }, { id: 2, name: 'Wireless Headphones', category: 'electronics', price: 1999, oldPrice: null, badge: 'BEST', image: 'https://via.placeholder.com/600x400?text=Headphone', rating: 5 }, ] // Get all products or filter by category / search app.get('/api/products', (req, res) => { const q = (req.query.q || '').toLowerCase() const cat = req.query.category let resList = products if (cat) resList = resList.filter(p => p.category === cat) if (q) resList = resList.filter(p => p.name.toLowerCase().includes(q)) res.json(resList) }) app.get('/api/products/:id', (req, res) => { const p = products.find(x => x.id === Number(req.params.id)) if (!p) return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Not found' }) res.json(p) }) // Simple admin add (no auth in starter) app.post('/api/products', (req, res) => { const { name, category, price, oldPrice, badge, image } = req.body const id = products.length ? Math.max(...products.map(p=>p.id))+1 : 1 const p = { id, name, category, price, oldPrice: oldPrice || null, badge: badge || '', image, rating: 0 } products.push(p) res.status(201).json(p) }) const port = process.env.PORT || 4000 app.listen(port, ()=>console.log('API running on http://localhost:' + port)) ``` Server package.json ```json name=server/package.json { "name": "linknest-server", "version": "0.1.0", "main": "index.js", "scripts": { "dev": "nodemon index.js", "start": "node index.js" }, "dependencies": { "cors": "^2.8.5", "express": "^4.18.2" }, "devDependencies": { "nodemon": "^2.0.22" } } ``` Client: React + Vite (minimal) ```javascript name=client/src/App.jsx import { useEffect, useState } from 'react' import './styles.css' function ProductCard({p, onAdd}) { return (
{p.badge && {p.badge}}

{p.name}

{'★'.repeat(p.rating)}{'☆'.repeat(5-p.rating)}
₹{p.price} {p.oldPrice && ₹{p.oldPrice}}
) } export default function App() { const [products, setProducts] = useState([]) const [cart, setCart] = useState([]) const [q, setQ] = useState('') useEffect(()=> { fetch('/api/products') .then(r=>r.json()) .then(setProducts) }, []) const addToCart = (p) => setCart(c => [...c, p]) const filtered = products.filter(p => p.name.toLowerCase().includes(q.toLowerCase())) return (

LinkNest

setQ(e.target.value)} />
Cart: {cart.length}
{filtered.map(p => )}
) } ``` Client package.json ```json name=client/package.json { "name": "linknest-client", "version": "0.1.0", "private": true, "scripts": { "dev": "vite", "build": "vite build", "preview": "vite preview" }, "dependencies": { "react": "^18.2.0", "react-dom": "^18.2.0" }, "devDependencies": { "vite": "^5.0.0" } } ``` Client index.html ```html name=client/public/index.html LinkNest
``` Client main entry (tiny) ```javascript name=client/src/main.jsx import React from 'react' import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client' import App from './App' import './styles.css' createRoot(document.getElementById('root')).render() ``` Client basic styles (you can reuse your earlier CSS) ```css name=client/src/styles.css *{box-sizing:border-box;font-family:system-ui,Arial} body{margin:0;background:#f1f3f6} .card{background:#fff;border-radius:6px;padding:10px;box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,.1)} .card img{width:100%;height:150px;object-fit:contain} .badge{position:relative;background:#388e3c;color:#fff;padding:3px 6px;font-size:12px;border-radius:4px} .buy{width:100%;padding:10px;background:#ff9f00;border:none;border-radius:4px;cursor:pointer} ``` What I can do next (pick one) - Create this scaffold and open a PR in your repo (I can push branch and PR). - Extend scaffold with Postgres + Prisma and auth (signup/login). - Integrate a payment gateway (Stripe or Paytm) and real checkout flow. - Build an admin panel + product upload with image storage to S3. - Convert frontend to Next.js for SEO/SSR. Tell me: - Which features you want in the MVP (short list), and - If you want me to push this starter into lavk7251-hue/LinkNest- as a branch/PR (if yes, confirm branch name like feature/mvp-starter). I'll start immediately once you confirm which path and the repo action.

MVP features:
- Public product listing with categories and search
- Affiliate Buy Now button (external links only)
- User authentication (email/password)
- Product submission form for users
- Admin approval before products go live
- Click tracking per product
- Basic estimated earnings dashboard
- SEO-friendly pages

Nice — thanks. Because you need SEO-friendly pages, admin workflows, auth, click tracking and affiliate redirects, I recommend building the MVP with Next.js (server-rendered pages for SEO) + PostgreSQL (Prisma ORM) + NextAuth (email/password via Credentials + bcrypt) and a small Express/Next API for tracking/redirects (we can implement entirely inside Next.js API routes). This stack gives server-side rendering, good SEO, easy deployment (Vercel + Railway/PlanetScale/Neon), and robust DB usage.

Below is a concrete implementation plan, DB schema, API & route design, click-tracking approach, estimated-earnings design, security notes, timeline, and the immediate next step I can take (scaffold + PR).

1) Tech stack (recommended)
- Frontend / framework: Next.js (App or Pages Router) — SSR/SSG for SEO-friendly pages
- Auth: NextAuth with a Credentials provider (email/password) and Prisma adapter (store hashed passwords using bcrypt)
- Database: PostgreSQL (hosted on Railway / Neon / PlanetScale)
- ORM: Prisma
- Storage: S3 or S3-compatible bucket for product images (or store image URLs initially)
- Hosting: Vercel for Next.js, Railway/Neon for Postgres
- Analytics/tracking: store in DB (clicks table). Optional export to analytics services later.
- Optional: Redis for sessions / rate-limiting

2) Core data models (Prisma-style)
- Product: holds affiliate URL (kept private), public fields, commission rate
- User: users with role (user/admin)
- Submission: product submissions by users (pending until admin approves)
- Click: tracking each redirect/click
- Earnings or Calculated metrics: not necessarily persisted — can compute on the fly from Clicks & Product commission

Example Prisma schema models (conceptual)
```prisma
model User {
  id        Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
  email     String   @unique
  password  String   // bcrypt-hash
  name      String?
  role      String   @default("user") // 'user' | 'admin'
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  submissions Submission[]
  clicks    Click[]
}

model Product {
  id             Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
  name           String
  slug           String   @unique
  description    String?
  category       String
  imageUrl       String?
  price          Float
  oldPrice       Float?
  badge          String?
  commissionPct  Float?   // e.g. 0.05 for 5%
  affiliateUrl   String   // encrypted or access-controlled; not exposed on client
  approved       Boolean  @default(false)
  createdById    Int?
  createdBy      User?    @relation(fields: [createdById], references: [id])
  createdAt      DateTime @default(now())
  clicks         Click[]
  submissions    Submission?
}

model Submission {
  id         Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
  productId  Int?     // optional link if admin converts submission -> product
  name       String
  affiliateUrl String
  category   String
  imageUrl   String?
  price      Float?
  submittedById Int?
  status     String   @default("pending") // pending | approved | rejected
  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())
  reviewedById Int?
  reviewedAt DateTime?
}

model Click {
  id        Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
  productId Int
  userId    Int?
  ip        String?
  userAgent String?
  referrer  String?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  product Product @relation(fields:[productId], references:[id])
  user    User?   @relation(fields:[userId], references:[id])
}
```

3) Routes / API endpoints
- Public pages (SEO friendly):
  - GET / — Home & categories (SSR)
  - GET /category/[slug] — Category listings (SSR)
  - GET /product/[slug] — Product detail page (SSR) — uses server-rendered meta tags (title, description, og:image)

- Client-side endpoints (Next.js API routes):
  - POST /api/auth/signup — create user (hash password)
  - POST /api/auth/login — NextAuth Credentials provider (or use NextAuth route)
  - GET /api/products — list products (with ?q= & ?category= & pagination)
  - GET /api/products/[id] — product data (for CSR if needed)
  - POST /api/submissions — create product submission (auth required)
  - GET /api/admin/submissions — admin only: list pending submissions
  - PATCH /api/admin/submissions/[id]/approve — admin approves (create Product from submission or mark approved)
  - GET /r/[productId]?token=... — redirect endpoint: logs a Click and redirects to affiliateUrl
    - Implementation detail: the public "Buy Now" anchor will point to /r/[slug] (server redirects to affiliateUrl after logging)
  - GET /api/admin/earnings — compute metrics: clicks per product, estimated earnings (see below)

4) Click tracking & affiliate redirect flow
- Public Buy Now links on product pages will point to internal redirect route /r/[slug] (or /r?id=) rather than exposing affiliateUrl.
- When user clicks:
  - Next.js API route (server-side) receives request, logs a Click entry with productId, ip, user-agent, referrer, (optionally userId if logged in).
  - Then responds with a 302 redirect to the affiliateUrl stored in DB.
- Benefits:
  - You can count clicks reliably
  - Affiliate URLs remain not directly visible in page source (obfuscation, though not full security)
  - Rate-limiting and validation possible (block malicious bots)
- Security/Privacy:
  - Validate affiliateUrl format when storing
  - Rate-limit redirect endpoint (to avoid abuse)
  - Store only necessary metadata (avoid logging full IP unless your privacy policy allows; consider hashing or truncating IP)

5) Estimated earnings model
- Two options:
  - Simple: estimatedEarnings = clicks * price * commissionPct * estimatedConversionRate
    - Let admin configure a default conversion rate (e.g., 1–2% typical for affiliate).
    - Show for each product: clicks last 30 days, est conversions (clicks * convRate), est earnings.
  - If you later get actual conversions/reports from affiliate program, allow manual reconciliation (upload CSV, map product -> actual commissions).
- Store commissionPct on product or per affiliate program.

6) Auth, roles & admin workflow
- Signup & login with email/password.
- Role field on User determines admin access. Admin routes will check session and role.
- Product submissions:
  - Authenticated users can POST to /api/submissions; submission created with status pending.
  - Admin dashboard lists pending submissions; admin can approve/convert to Product or reject with feedback.

7) SEO & performance
- Use Next.js SSR for product & category pages to ensure open graph tags and good SEO.
- Use slug-based product URLs: /product/5g-smartphone-123
- Implement sitemap.xml and robots.txt
- Use image optimization (Next/Image or pre-signed CDN images)
- Server-side caching for high-traffic category pages (ISR or cache headers)

8) Basic UI pages (minimum to implement)
- Public:
  - Home (featured categories)
  - Category list page (with search + filters)
  - Product page (with Buy Now button -> /r/[slug])
  - Search results page
  - Signup/login pages
  - Submit product form (authenticated)
- Admin:
  - Login (admin uses same auth)
  - Submissions list & detail (approve/reject)
  - Products list (manage)
  - Earnings dashboard (clicks & estimated earnings)

9) Security & best practices
- Hash passwords using bcrypt with adequate cost.
- Use CSRF protection for form submissions (NextAuth + secure cookies).
- Use HTTPS and HSTS.
- Validate, sanitize all user input (submission info).
- Rate-limit API endpoints (especially /r redirect and submission endpoints).
- Use environment variables for secrets (DATABASE_URL, NEXTAUTH_SECRET, S3 keys).
- Ensure affiliate urls are validated on input (only allow http/https).

10) Env / configuration
- Environment variables to set:
  - DATABASE_URL
  - NEXTAUTH_SECRET
  - NEXTAUTH_URL
  - S3_BUCKET, S3_KEY, S3_SECRET (if used)
  - ADMIN_EMAIL (initial admin) — or create via seed script
  - DEFAULT_CONVERSION_RATE (e.g., 0.01)

11) Timeline & milestones (rough)
- Day 0: Confirm stack & repo permissions
- Day 1: Scaffold Next.js + Prisma, seed DB, basic product listing + SSR pages + sitemap
- Day 2: Implement NextAuth email/password signup/login (with Prisma)
- Day 3: Implement product submission form and submission API + admin submissions list UI
- Day 4: Implement redirect endpoint / click logging + Buy Now links + basic earnings dashboard
- Day 5: Polish UX, add search & filters, add SEO tags, prepare deployment
Total: ~5 working days for the MVP (can be split into smaller PRs each day)

12) Dev tasks & PR plan (incremental)
- PR 1: Project scaffold (Next.js + Prisma + initial DB & seed data, home/category/product listing pages)
- PR 2: Auth (signup/login) + user session management
- PR 3: Submission API + Submit UI + admin Pending list
- PR 4: Redirect endpoint + click logging + Buy Now UI change
- PR 5: Earnings dashboard + minor polishing + sitemap + deployment config

13) What I can do immediately
Pick one of these and I will start right away:
A) Create the full scaffold and push a branch + open a PR in your repo lavk7251-hue/LinkNest- (I will:
   - Create Next.js app
   - Setup Prisma + Postgres schema + seed
   - Implement product listing + product page SSR + redirect endpoint stub
   - Add README with run instructions)
B) Implement Auth first (signup/login + sessions) as a standalone PR
C) Implement redirect & click-tracking endpoint and wire Buy Now buttons to it
D) Provide a minimal code scaffold here (files) so you can run locally (I can paste file blocks)

If you want me to push code into your repository, please confirm:
- You want me to create a branch (suggested name: feature/mvp-nextjs) and open a PR
- I have permission to push changes (I will create commits under my bot account in the branch)
- Any preferences for hosting (Vercel + Railway, or something else)

Also tell me:
- Do you want me to use Next.js App Router or Pages Router? (I suggest App Router if you're comfortable; otherwise Pages Router is fine)
- Do you want image uploads to S3 in MVP, or is an image URL enough for now?

Once you confirm decisions above (Next.js + Prisma recommended, branch name, hosting preference, image upload preference), I’ll scaffold the repo and start with PR #1 (scaffold + product listing + redirect).

Go ahead with the full MVP scaffold.

Decisions:
- Stack: Next.js + Prisma (Postgres)
- Router: Next.js App Router
- Auth: NextAuth (email/password)
- Image handling: image URLs only for MVP (no S3 for now)
- Hosting preference: Vercel

Repo actions:
- Create a new branch named: feature/mvp-nextjs
- Open a PR with the initial scaffold, product listing pages, redirect endpoint, and basic SEO setup.
- You have permission to push commits to the branch.

Please start with PR #1: scaffold + product listing (SSR) + redirect endpoint + sitemap.
I'm ready to create the branch and open the PR — I'm waiting for you to accept the repository access dialog so I can continue.

I tried creating the branch in your repository but the repository is currently empty, so I couldn't push the branch from here. I can’t push changes directly until the repo has an initial default branch. Below I’ve prepared the full MVP scaffold (Next.js App Router + Prisma) with product listing (SSR), redirect endpoint for affiliate clicks (/r/[slug]), and a sitemap route. You can add these files to the repo and push a branch named feature/mvp-nextjs, or follow the instructions to initialize the repo and create the PR. What I did - Built a minimal but complete Next.js App Router scaffold that uses Prisma for DB access. - Implemented server-rendered product listing (home page), product pages with SEO metadata, an internal redirect route that logs clicks and redirects to the affiliate URL, and a dynamic sitemap.xml route. Next actions I need from you - Initialize the repository (create a default branch like main) OR run the git steps below locally to create the branch and push the scaffold. - Provide your DATABASE_URL and NEXTAUTH_SECRET (for later PRs) in GitHub Secrets when deploying to Vercel. - After you push the branch, I can open the PR with these changes. Files (add these to the repo under the given paths) ```json name=package.json { "name": "linknest-mvp", "version": "0.1.0", "private": true, "scripts": { "dev": "next dev", "build": "next build", "start": "next start", "prisma:generate": "prisma generate", "prisma:migrate": "prisma migrate dev --name init", "prisma:seed": "node prisma/seed.js" }, "dependencies": { "@prisma/client": "^5.0.0", "next": "14.0.0", "react": "18.2.0", "react-dom": "18.2.0", "next-auth": "^4.22.1", "bcrypt": "^5.1.0" }, "devDependencies": { "prisma": "^5.0.0" } } ``` ```text name=.env.example # Rename to .env.local and set real values DATABASE_URL="postgresql://user:password@localhost:5432/linknest?schema=public" NEXTAUTH_SECRET="replace_with_a_strong_random_value" NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL="http://localhost:3000" ``` ```prisma name=prisma/schema.prisma generator client { provider = "prisma-client-js" } datasource db { provider = "postgresql" url = env("DATABASE_URL") } model User { id Int @id @default(autoincrement()) email String @unique password String name String? role String @default("user") // user | admin createdAt DateTime @default(now()) Submissions Submission[] Click Click[] } model Product { id Int @id @default(autoincrement()) name String slug String @unique description String? category String imageUrl String? price Float oldPrice Float? badge String? commissionPct Float? // e.g. 0.05 affiliateUrl String approved Boolean @default(false) createdById Int? createdBy User? @relation(fields: [createdById], references: [id]) createdAt DateTime @default(now()) Click Click[] } model Submission { id Int @id @default(autoincrement()) name String slug String @unique affiliateUrl String category String imageUrl String? price Float? submittedBy Int? status String @default("pending") // pending | approved | rejected createdAt DateTime @default(now()) reviewedById Int? reviewedAt DateTime? } model Click { id Int @id @default(autoincrement()) productId Int userId Int? ip String? userAgent String? referrer String? createdAt DateTime @default(now()) product Product @relation(fields:[productId], references:[id]) user User? @relation(fields:[userId], references:[id]) } ``` ```javascript name=prisma/seed.js const { PrismaClient } = require('@prisma/client') const prisma = new PrismaClient() async function main() { await prisma.product.createMany({ data: [ { name: '5G Smartphone', slug: '5g-smartphone', description: 'Fast 5G smartphone with great camera.', category: 'mobile', imageUrl: 'https://via.placeholder.com/600x400?text=Phone', price: 14999, oldPrice: 18999, badge: '20% OFF', commissionPct: 0.05, affiliateUrl: 'https://dl.flipkart.com/s/YOUR_LINK', approved: true }, { name: 'Wireless Headphones', slug: 'wireless-headphones', description: 'Comfortable wireless headphones with long battery life.', category: 'electronics', imageUrl: 'https://via.placeholder.com/600x400?text=Headphone', price: 1999, badge: 'BEST', commissionPct: 0.07, affiliateUrl: 'https://dl.flipkart.com/s/YOUR_LINK', approved: true } ] }) console.log('Seeded products.') } main() .catch((e) => { console.error(e) process.exit(1) }) .finally(async () => { await prisma.$disconnect() }) ``` ```javascript name=lib/prisma.ts import { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client' declare global { // eslint-disable-next-line no-var var prisma: PrismaClient | undefined } const prisma = global.prisma || new PrismaClient() if (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development') global.prisma = prisma export default prisma ``` ```tsx name=app/layout.tsx import './globals.css' import React from 'react' export const metadata = { title: 'LinkNest – Smart Shopping', description: 'Curated affiliate shopping links and deals' } export default function RootLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) { return (

LinkNest

{children}
Affiliate disclosure: Links may earn commission • LinkNest
) } ``` ```tsx name=app/page.tsx import prisma from '../lib/prisma' import Link from 'next/link' export default async function Home() { const products = await prisma.product.findMany({ where: { approved: true }, orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' } }) return (
{products.map(p => (
{p.badge &&
{p.badge}
}

{p.name}

₹{p.price} {p.oldPrice ? ₹{p.oldPrice} : null}
Buy Now Details
))}
) } ``` ```tsx name=app/product/[slug]/page.tsx import prisma from '../../../lib/prisma' import { notFound } from 'next/navigation' import Link from 'next/link' type Props = { params: { slug: string } } export async function generateMetadata({ params }: Props) { const product = await prisma.product.findUnique({ where: { slug: params.slug } }) if (!product) return { title: 'Product not found' } return { title: `${product.name} — LinkNest`, description: product.description || `${product.name} on LinkNest`, openGraph: { images: product.imageUrl ? [product.imageUrl] : [] } } } export default async function ProductPage({ params }: Props) { const product = await prisma.product.findUnique({ where: { slug: params.slug } }) if (!product || !product.approved) notFound() return (

{product.name}

₹{product.price} {product.oldPrice ? ₹{product.oldPrice} : null}

{product.description}

Buy Now
) } ``` ```ts name=app/api/r/[slug]/route.ts import { NextResponse } from 'next/server' import prisma from '../../../../lib/prisma' export async function GET(request: Request, { params }: { params: { slug: string } }) { const { slug } = params const product = await prisma.product.findUnique({ where: { slug } }) if (!product || !product.approved) { return new NextResponse('Not found', { status: 404 }) } // Try to capture some metadata const ua = request.headers.get('user-agent') || undefined const ref = request.headers.get('referer') || undefined // X-Forwarded-For may contain client IP const ip = request.headers.get('x-forwarded-for') || undefined try { await prisma.click.create({ data: { productId: product.id, userAgent: ua, referrer: ref, ip } }) } catch (err) { // Logging failure must not block redirect console.error('Click log error', err) } return NextResponse.redirect(product.affiliateUrl, 302) } ``` ```ts name=app/sitemap.xml/route.ts import prisma from '../../lib/prisma' export async function GET() { const products = await prisma.product.findMany({ where: { approved: true } }) const baseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL || 'https://example.com' const urls = products.map(p => ` ${baseUrl}/product/${p.slug} ${p.createdAt.toISOString()} weekly 0.8 `).join('') const xml = ` ${baseUrl} daily 1.0 ${urls} ` return new Response(xml, { headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/xml' } }) } ``` ```css name=app/globals.css :root { --bg: #f1f3f6 } *{box-sizing:border-box;font-family:system-ui,Arial} body{margin:0;background:var(--bg)} a{color:inherit} ``` ````markdown name=README.md ```markdown # LinkNest — MVP scaffold (Next.js + Prisma) This scaffold provides: - Next.js (App Router) with SSR product listing and product pages (SEO friendly) - Prisma schema (Postgres) - Redirect endpoint (/r/[slug]) that logs clicks and redirects to the affiliate URL - Sitemap route at /sitemap.xml How to run locally 1. Clone the repo and create the branch: (If the repo is empty, create main first — see notes below) 2. Install dependencies: npm install 3. Create .env.local from .env.example and set DATABASE_URL and NEXTAUTH_SECRET 4. Generate Prisma client & run migration: npx prisma generate npx prisma migrate dev --name init 5. Seed initial products: node prisma/seed.js 6. Option B — Initialize and push from your machine 1. Clone the empty repo: git clone git@github.com:lavk7251-hue/LinkNest-.git cd LinkNest- 2. Create a default branch and initial commit: git checkout -b main echo "# LinkNest" > README.md git add README.md git commit -m "chore: initialize main branch" git push origin main 3. Create the feature branch and add scaffold: git checkout -b feature/mvp-nextjs (copy scaffold files into the repo) git add . git commit -m "feat(mvp): initial Next.js + Prisma scaffold with product listing and redirect" git push -u origin feature/mvp-nextjs 4. Open a Pull Request on GitHub from feature/mvp-nextjs -> main. Deploying to Vercel - Connect the repo to Vercel. - Set environment variables in Vercel: DATABASE_URL, NEXTAUTH_SECRET, NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL. - Deploy. Which would you prefer? If you want me to open the PR, please create the default branch (a single README commit is sufficient) and I’ll push the feature/mvp-nextjs branch and open the PR immediately. ``` ```` How to create branch & open PR (quick commands) - Initialize main if empty (one-time): git checkout -b main echo "# LinkNest" > README.md git add README.md git commit -m "chore: init main" git push origin main - Add scaffold, create feature branch, push, open PR: git checkout -b feature/mvp-nextjs (copy files into repo) git add . git commit -m "feat(mvp): Next.js App Router scaffold, product listing, redirect, sitemap" git push -u origin feature/mvp-nextjs Then open a PR against main from the GitHub UI (or use gh cli: gh pr create --title "MVP scaffold" --body "Initial scaffold") If you want, I can: - Wait for you to initialize main and then push & open PR #1, or - Walk you step-by-step while you run the git commands and I’ll verify the PR once it exists. README.md.name: README.md
README.md.value:
# LinkNest Starter (Flipkart-like MVP)

This is a minimal starter scaffold (React frontend + Express backend).

Prereqs
- Node.js 18+
- npm or pnpm

Run locally
1. Start backend
   cd server
   npm install
   npm run dev
   (server runs at http://localhost:4000)

2. Start frontend
   cd client
   npm install
   npm run dev
   (client runs at http://localhost:5173)

What's included
- /server: Express API with in-memory product list (replace with Postgres)
- /client: React + Vite app showing product listing, product detail, simple cart

Next steps
- Replace in-memory store with PostgreSQL + ORM
- Add user auth (JWT/session)
- Add payments (Stripe)
- Add admin UI and image uploads

README.md.name: README.md
README.md.value:
```markdown
# LinkNest — MVP scaffold (Next.js + Prisma)

This scaffold provides:
- Next.js (App Router) with SSR product listing and product pages (SEO friendly)
- Prisma schema (Postgres)
- Redirect endpoint (/r/[slug]) that logs clicks and redirects to the affiliate URL
- Sitemap route at /sitemap.xml

How to run locally

1. Clone the repo and create the branch:
   (If the repo is empty, create main first — see notes below)

2. Install dependencies:
   npm install

3. Create .env.local from .env.example and set DATABASE_URL and NEXTAUTH_SECRET

4. Generate Prisma client & run migration:
   npx prisma generate
   npx prisma migrate dev --name init

5. Seed initial products:
   node prisma/seed.js

6. Run the dev server:
   npm run dev
   (Open http://localhost:3000)

Notes about GitHub repo initialization

If your GitHub repository is empty, do one of the following:

Option A — Initialize repo with a README (quick via GitHub UI)
- On the repository page, click "Add README" or initialize with a README to create the default branch (main).
- Then clone locally and add the scaffold files, create branch feature/mvp-nextjs, commit and push, then open a PR.

Option B — Initialize and push from your machine
1. Clone the empty repo:
   git clone git@github.com:lavk7251-hue/LinkNest-.git
   cd LinkNest-

2. Create a default branch and initial commit:
   git checkout -b main
   echo "# LinkNest" > README.md
   git add README.md
   git commit -m "chore: initialize main branch"
   git push origin main

3. Create the feature branch and add scaffold:
   git checkout -b feature/mvp-nextjs
   (copy scaffold files into the repo)
   git add .
   git commit -m "feat(mvp): initial Next.js + Prisma scaffold with product listing and redirect"
   git push -u origin feature/mvp-nextjs

4. Open a Pull Request on GitHub from feature/mvp-nextjs -> main.

Deploying to Vercel
- Connect the repo to Vercel.
- Set environment variables in Vercel: DATABASE_URL, NEXTAUTH_SECRET, NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL.
- Deploy.

Next steps I will implement after PR #1:
- NextAuth email/password signup & login with Prisma adapter.
- Product submission endpoints & user submission UI.
- Admin approval UI & endpoints.
- Click analytics dashboard / estimated earnings page.

If you want, I can continue and open PR #1 once you either:
- Create the default branch in the repo (main) OR
- Push the feature/mvp-nextjs branch yourself using the steps above.

Which would you prefer? If you want me to open the PR, please create the default branch (a single README commit is sufficient) and I’ll push the feature/mvp-nextjs branch and open the PR immediately.
```

DATABASE_URL="postgresql://user:password@localhost:5432/linknest?schema=public"
NEXTAUTH_SECRET="replace_with_a_strong_random_value"

prisma/schema.prisma.name: prisma/schema.prisma
prisma/schema.prisma.value:
generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

model User {
  id        Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
  email     String   @unique
  password  String
  name      String?
  role      String   @default("user") // user | admin
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  Submissions Submission[]
  Click      Click[]
}

model Product {
  id            Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
  name          String
  slug          String   @unique
  description   String?
  category      String
  imageUrl      String?
  price         Float
  oldPrice      Float?
  badge         String?
  commissionPct Float?   // e.g. 0.05
  affiliateUrl  String
  approved      Boolean  @default(false)
  createdById   Int?
  createdBy     User?    @relation(fields: [createdById], references: [id])
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())
  Click         Click[]
}

model Submission {
  id           Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
  name         String
  slug         String   @unique
  affiliateUrl String
  category     String
  imageUrl     String?
  price        Float?
  submittedBy  Int?
  status       String   @default("pending") // pending | approved | rejected
  createdAt    DateTime @default(now())
  reviewedById Int?
  reviewedAt   DateTime?
}

model Click {
  id        Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
  productId Int
  userId    Int?
  ip        String?
  userAgent String?
  referrer  String?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  product Product @relation(fields:[productId], references:[id])
  user    User?   @relation(fields:[userId], references:[id])
}
